curated.context: Bold contrast — warm orange against cool teal. Strong for CTAs and hero sections.
Grenadine Pink with Deep Indigo — dramatic feminine palette with high contrast.
Spinel Red with Naples Yellow — warm complementary pair with vintage charm.
Cameo Pink, Fawn, and Dusky Madder Violet — soft muted trio with depth.
Pure Yellow with Yellow Orange and Deep Lyons Blue — primary triad energy.
Etruscan Red with Nile Blue — warm red cooled by mint. Balanced tension.
Yellow Ocher and Violet — classic complementary with artistic gravitas.
Light Porcelain Green and Olympic Blue — cool analogous, trustworthy.
White and Old Rose — minimal and refined. Excellent for luxury.
Olympic Blue and Dark Tyrian Blue — monochromatic blue depth.
Seashell Pink and Deep Slate Green — nature-inspired contrast.
Pale Lemon Yellow and Benzol Green — fresh spring energy.
Cameo Pink, Fawn, Cerulian Blue, Violet Blue — rich quad with depth.
Carmine Red, Raw Sienna, Violet — earthy triad with punch.
Orange, Yellow Green, Dark Tyrian Blue — high-energy triad.
Spinel Red, Vandyke Red, Turquoise Green — dramatic with relief.
Pansy Purple, Olive Ocher, Olympic Blue — unexpected harmony.
Jasper Red, Seashell Pink, Olympic Blue — patriotic warmth.
Burnt Sienna, Apricot Yellow, Green — natural vitality.
Carmine, Pinkish Cinnamon, Deep Indigo — warm to cold gradient.
Eosine Pink, Burnt Sienna, Diamine Green, Black — rich quad.
Sea Green, Peach Red, Nile Blue, Pyrite Yellow — vibrant quad.
Corinthian Pink, Red Orange, Cerulian Blue, Dark Greenish Glaucous — balanced quad.
Light Brown Drab, Peach Red, Turquoise Green, Burnt Sienna — earthy warmth.
Raw Sienna, Turquoise Green, Artemesia Green, Green — nature palette.
Cameo Pink, Fawn, Scarlet, English Red, Cobalt Green — rich 5-color.
Spectrum Red, Brick Red, Blue Violet — deep contrast triad.
Coral Red, Scarlet, Deep Slate Olive, Nile Blue, Dusky Green — complex.
Sea Green, Grenadine Pink, Black, Neutral Gray — modern contrast.
Cossack Green, Olive Buff, Deep Slate Olive, Cotinga Purple — deep earth.
